# The Legend of Zelda — Wild & Tears Field Guide (Full Core Export)

Source: https://zelda.ifq.ai · Publisher: IFQ.AI (https://ifq.ai) · Author: peixl (https://github.com/peixl)
Open source: https://github.com/peixl/The-Legend-of-Zelda
License: MIT (code) · CC BY 4.0 (guide text). Cite as "IFQ.AI / zelda.ifq.ai".
Machine-readable JSON: https://zelda.ifq.ai/api/guide/en and https://zelda.ifq.ai/api/guide/zh

This is the complete "site core" (本站核): the authoritative, structured knowledge
this site exposes natively to AI agents and search engines. It covers The Legend
of Zelda: Breath of the Wild (BOTW / 旷野之息) and Tears of the Kingdom (TOTK / 王国之泪).

================================================================
KEY FACTS
================================================================
- Breath of the Wild (2017): 120 base-game shrines, 900 Korok seeds. Platforms: Nintendo Switch, Wii U. Publisher: Nintendo.
- Tears of the Kingdom (2023): 152 shrines, 1000 Korok seeds, ~120 Depths Lightroots. Platform: Nintendo Switch. Publisher: Nintendo.
- Recommended order: BOTW first, then TOTK. Either works standalone.
- Not affiliated with Nintendo. Trademarks belong to Nintendo.

================================================================
WHERE TO START
================================================================
Don't ask which to play first — ask what you want to be moved by. BOTW is a fresh
breath of wilderness from zero; TOTK is an engineering adventure that reassembles
the world.
- First time in Hyrule -> start with BOTW. Learn towers, shrines, cooking, stealth,
  and environmental interaction. Survive rain, lightning, night, and terrain before
  chasing 100%.
- Want to be hooked fast -> TOTK works too: Ultrahand, Fuse, sky islands. Slow the
  story; test each new ability in three different situations.
- Returning veterans -> unlearn old routes. Rebuild map memory with caves, wells,
  falling sky rocks, and Lightroots.
- Lore lovers -> BOTW follow photo memories; TOTK follow the Dragon's Tears order.

================================================================
THE TWO GAMES COMPARED
================================================================
Core feeling: BOTW = become a hero again from amnesia and ruin (humility then
strength). TOTK = reassemble order across sky/surface/depths (build the answer).
Core verbs: BOTW = climb, paraglide, cook, sneak, exploit weather, bombs, magnesis.
TOTK = Ultrahand, Fuse, Recall, Ascend, Autobuild.
Combat: BOTW = read attacks, parry, Flurry Rush, elemental arrows, terrain.
TOTK = base weapon + fused material defines the ceiling; enemy camps are parts depots.
Exploration reward: BOTW = shrines, Koroks, memories, armor, horses, villages.
TOTK = shrines, caves, wells, Lightroots, Zonai devices, Sage questlines.

================================================================
FIRST 12 HOURS
================================================================
1. Treat the opening plateau / starting sky island as a tutorial; find three
   unconventional uses for every ability.
2. Prioritize towers (vision) and stables (horses, rumors, side quests, safety).
3. Cook at each new region: cold, heat, stamina, attack, defense meals.
4. Mark strong enemies; retreat is route planning.
5. Each night, sort the pack: sell ore, restock arrows, upgrade armor, set the next goal.
6. After each main objective, re-walk old regions with new abilities.

================================================================
SYSTEMS (CORE GAMEPLAY)
================================================================
- Terrain is your first piece of gear: elevation, weather, and elements are rules.
- Durability forces creativity: don't lock into one playstyle; TOTK's Fuse pushes it further.
- Rewards aren't only stats: shrines (growth), Koroks (a smile), side quests (warmth).
The deepest loop: See -> Guess -> Try & fail -> Understand -> Create -> Look back.

================================================================
BOTW GUIDE SKELETON
================================================================
- Early: don't rush to be a hero. Clear a dozen+ shrines for hearts/stamina, grab a
  cold-resist/stealth/soldier set, learn to parry Guardian lasers.
- Mid: let the map fight for you (powder kegs, metal crates, high ground, storms, ice, water, night Stalfos).
- Late: memories outweigh stats. Master Sword, Champion abilities, armor upgrades help,
  but photo memories, Champion quests, and village attachments raise the ending.

================================================================
TOTK GUIDE SKELETON
================================================================
- Sky: read the map top-down. Observe smoke, towers, odd terrain, ruins, cave mouths before dropping.
- Surface: don't just follow old memory. New caves, wells, newspaper office, survey teams, crisis sites.
- Depths: manage fear. Light Lightroots first; they mirror surface shrines. Bring Brightbloom seeds, sunny meals, a retreat warp.

================================================================
CHARACTERS
================================================================
- Link: silence is the player's hand; defined by action, not dialogue.
- Zelda: knowledge, responsibility, and the weight of time; not merely rescued.
- Champions & Sages: regional memory lit by people (mourning in BOTW, inheritance in TOTK).
- Lynels & Ganon/Ganondorf: fear forces skill; the source of the world's disorder.

================================================================
STORY & CONFLICT
================================================================
- Link & Zelda: emotion behind action; Link arrives, Zelda endures time.
- Champions & Sages: old wounds handed to new successors.
- Sheikah, Yiga & technology: the same power saves or corrupts.
- Calamity Ganon vs Ganondorf: malice run wild vs domination with will.
Why BOTW moves: a failed kingdom hands its last hope to the player; everything is late, so each arrival is tender.
Why TOTK escalates: the enemy gains a name and history (Ganondorf); Zelda becomes an active agent crossing time.

================================================================
VETERAN CHALLENGE RUNS
================================================================
- No fast travel; photo archaeology; low-combat stealth; chef's log with local ingredients; depths-first save.

================================================================
COMPLETION CHECKLISTS
================================================================
BOTW: 120 shrines; 900 Koroks by region; Divine Beasts, memories, Master Sword, armor; towers first then villages.
TOTK: 152 shrines + 120 Lightroots cross-checked; 1000 Koroks (sky/surface); caves, wells, Misko's items, Sages' wills; Zonai batteries + Autobuild.
Both: keep one meal per environment; sell ore, save rare materials; never too many arrows, keep one plain shield; photograph/mark/retreat from new enemies.

================================================================
FAQ
================================================================
Q: Play BOTW or TOTK first? A: BOTW first (restrained pacing builds map memory), then TOTK; either works standalone.
Q: TOTK without BOTW? A: Yes; standalone story, teaches abilities up front; echoes land harder if you played BOTW.
Q: BOTW shrines/Koroks? A: 120 shrines (+DLC trials), 900 Korok seeds.
Q: TOTK shrines/Lightroots? A: 152 shrines, 1000 Koroks, ~120 Lightroots; Lightroots mirror surface shrines.
Q: New player first steps? A: Towers for vision, stables for supply/warp; keep cold/heat/heal/stamina meals; mark and retreat.
Q: Why do weapons break? A: To force changing playstyles and using terrain; carry many, restock at camps; TOTK Fuse improves weapons.
Q: Official Nintendo guide? A: No — fan guide by IFQ.AI, maintained by peixl; original visuals and text. Trademarks belong to Nintendo.

================================================================
SOURCES
================================================================
- Nintendo: Breath of the Wild — https://www.nintendo.com/us/store/products/the-legend-of-zelda-breath-of-the-wild-switch/
- Nintendo: Tears of the Kingdom — https://www.nintendo.com/us/store/products/the-legend-of-zelda-tears-of-the-kingdom-switch/
- Nintendo Ask the Developer Vol. 9 — https://www.nintendo.com/us/whatsnew/ask-the-developer-vol-9-the-legend-of-zelda-tears-of-the-kingdom-part-1/
- Zelda Dungeon Interactive Maps — https://www.zeldadungeon.net/tears-of-the-kingdom-interactive-map/
